summary: Published self-hosted Langfuse at https://langfuse.lv3.org, verified the seeded lv3-agent-observability project through the public API, and ingested a public smoke trace that resolved in the Langfuse UI after fixing topology generation, edge publication recovery defaults, and Redis persistence ownership.

verification.check: Repository generation, syntax, and focused regression coverage
verification.observed: Ran `make generate-platform-vars`, `make syntax-check-langfuse`, and `uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml python -m pytest tests/test_langfuse_observability.py tests/test_keycloak_runtime_role.py tests/test_compose_runtime_secret_injection.py tests/test_generate_platform_vars.py tests/test_nginx_edge_publication_role.py -q`, which finished with `29 passed in 0.65s`.
From the controller on 2026-03-26, `curl -fsS -D - https://langfuse.lv3.org/api/public/health` returned `HTTP/2 200` with body `{"status":"OK","version":"3.162.0"}`.

Using the controller-local public and secret project keys, `curl -fsS -u "$LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY:$LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY" https://langfuse.lv3.org/api/public/projects` returned project `lv3-agent-observability` in organization `lv3`.

verification.check: Public smoke trace and UI resolution
verification.observed: Running `uv run --with langfuse --with requests python scripts/langfuse_trace_smoke.py --base-url https://langfuse.lv3.org --project-id lv3-agent-observability --bootstrap-email baditaflorin@gmail.com --bootstrap-password-file .local/langfuse/bootstrap-user-password.txt` returned `api_verified=true`, `ui_verified=true`, trace id `4080b556f5c041e3a6afc28f37b99d41`, and trace URL `https://langfuse.lv3.org/project/lv3-agent-observability/traces/4080b556f5c041e3a6afc28f37b99d41`.

notes: The final live rollout depended on adding canonical `lv3_service_topology.langfuse` source data so generated platform vars exposed the public hostname, URLs, and internal port contract.

Edge publication recovery from `--start-at-task` required moving `public_edge_certbot_plugin_path` into role defaults so certificate expansion and nginx config convergence can resume safely after partial applies.

Trace ingestion initially failed with Redis `MISCONF` errors because the host-mounted Redis data directory was owned by root; pinning the directory and container runtime to Redis UID/GID `999` restored background snapshot persistence and trace writes.
